title: Normalizing and denoising protein expression data from droplet-based single-cell profiling
abstract: We present dsb, a method that uses empty droplets and isotype controls to remove ambient noise and cell-to-cell technical variation from antibody-derived tag (ADT) counts in CITE-seq. dsb improves clustering and cell-type separation relative to log or CLR normalization.

title: Joint probabilistic modeling of single-cell multi-omic data with totalVI
abstract: totalVI is a deep generative model that jointly analyzes paired transcriptome and surface-protein (CITE-seq) measurements, accounting for protein background and batch effects, and enabling integration, imputation, and differential expression across modalities.

title: Single-cell profiling reveals APOE4-driven microglial states in Alzheimer's disease
abstract: Using single-cell RNA sequencing of postmortem human cortex, we identify disease-associated microglial subpopulations enriched in APOE4 carriers, with upregulation of lipid-metabolism and complement genes, linking the APOE4 allele to a distinct neuroinflammatory microglial program.

title: CITE-seq of human brain border-associated macrophages and microglia
abstract: We apply CITE-seq with a 60-antibody panel to human brain immune cells, resolving microglia, border-associated macrophages, and infiltrating monocytes by joint surface-protein and transcriptomic signatures, and provide normalized ADT references for brain myeloid populations.

title: muon: multimodal omics analysis in the scverse ecosystem
abstract: muon extends anndata to multimodal single-cell data including CITE-seq and multiome, providing data structures and workflows for paired RNA and surface-protein analysis, integrated with scanpy and scvi-tools.

title: A CRISPR-Cas9 screen identifies drought-tolerance loci in maize
abstract: We performed a genome-wide CRISPR knockout screen in maize seedlings under water-limited conditions, identifying 42 loci whose disruption alters stomatal density and water-use efficiency, with implications for crop engineering.

title: A phase III randomized trial of a once-weekly GLP-1 agonist in type 2 diabetes
abstract: In 1,842 patients with type 2 diabetes, the GLP-1 receptor agonist reduced HbA1c by 1.4% versus placebo over 52 weeks, with significant weight loss and a favorable cardiovascular safety profile.

title: Cryo-EM structure of the bacterial 70S ribosome at 2.1 angstrom resolution
abstract: We report a high-resolution cryo-electron microscopy structure of the E. coli 70S ribosome, resolving solvent molecules and ion-binding sites in the peptidyl-transferase center and revealing conformational dynamics during translation.

title: Single-cell ATAC-seq maps chromatin accessibility in Arabidopsis root tips
abstract: We generated single-cell ATAC-seq profiles of Arabidopsis thaliana root cells, identifying cell-type-specific accessible regions and transcription-factor motifs governing root development. No protein or transcriptome modality was profiled.

title: Functional MRI of language switching in bilingual adults
abstract: Using task-based fMRI in 36 bilingual participants, we find that language switching engages left prefrontal and anterior cingulate cortex, supporting a domain-general control account of bilingual language selection.

title: Single-cell RNA sequencing of mouse microglia across the lifespan
abstract: We profile mouse microglia by scRNA-seq from postnatal to aged timepoints, identifying age-associated microglial states with interferon and lipid signatures. The study uses transcriptome only, with no surface-protein measurement, and is performed in mouse.

title: APOE locus variants and coronary artery disease risk in a large GWAS
abstract: A genome-wide association meta-analysis of 400,000 individuals links APOE region variants to LDL cholesterol and coronary artery disease, characterizing lipid-mediated cardiovascular risk. The study concerns cardiovascular disease, not the brain, and uses bulk genotyping.

title: Fast, sensitive integration of single-cell data with Harmony
abstract: Harmony is a scalable algorithm for integrating single-cell datasets across batches and conditions by iterative clustering in a corrected embedding. It is modality-agnostic and widely used for scRNA-seq, but is not specific to surface protein or CITE-seq.

title: High-dimensional flow cytometry immunophenotyping of human microglia
abstract: Using a 28-marker spectral flow cytometry panel, we characterize surface-protein heterogeneity of microglia isolated from human brain, defining activation states by CD markers. The study measures surface protein but uses flow cytometry, not single-cell sequencing.

title: CITE-seq of peripheral blood mononuclear cells in systemic lupus erythematosus
abstract: We apply CITE-seq with a 130-antibody panel to PBMCs from lupus patients and controls, mapping surface-protein and transcriptomic signatures of B-cell and monocyte activation. The method is CITE-seq, but the tissue is blood and the disease is lupus, not a brain or APOE context.
